examples.title: Weighted K-Nearest Neighbor Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode for Weighted KNN classification and regression
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WeightedKNN
INPUT: query_point, training_data, k
OUTPUT: predicted_class or predicted_value

BEGIN
 // Calculate distances between query_point and all training_data points
 distances = calculate_distances(query_point, training_data)

 // Find k nearest neighbors
 k_nearest_neighbors = find_k_nearest_neighbors(distances, k)

 // Assign weights to each neighbor
 weights = assign_weights(k_nearest_neighbors)

 // Classification
 IF classification THEN
 predicted_class = weighted_vote(k_nearest_neighbors, weights)
 ELSE // Regression
 predicted_value = weighted_average(k_nearest_neighbors, weights)
 END IF

 RETURN predicted_class or predicted_value
END
examples.explanation: Weighted KNN pseudocode for classification and regression tasks

examples.title: Weighted K-Nearest Neighbor Algorithm in Python
examples.description: Python implementation of Weighted KNN for classification and regression
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial import distance

def weighted_knn(query_point, training_data, k):
 distances = [distance.euclidean(query_point, point) for point in training_data]
 k_nearest_neighbors = np.argsort(distances)[:k]
 weights = [1 / (distances[i] ** 2) for i in k_nearest_neighbors]

 # Classification
 if classification:
 classes = [training_data[i][1] for i in k_nearest_neighbors]
 predicted_class = np.argmax(np.bincount(classes, weights=weights))
 else: # Regression
 values = [training_data[i][1] for i in k_nearest_neighbors]
 predicted_value = np.average(values, weights=weights)

 return predicted_class or predicted_value
examples.explanation: Python implementation of Weighted KNN for classification and regression tasks
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting distances
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing distances and weights
